place: Park Ridge borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Park Ridge was created as a borough on May 15, 1894, after a referendum the day before.
The town’s first train station opened in 1912 on the Delaware, Lackawanna and Western Railroad, linking residents to New York City.
The Basking Ridge Presbyterian Church, built in 1839 in Greek Revival style, sits on a site that later earned National Register status in 1974.
William Annin Middle School traces its name to a colonial patriot who settled in the area in 1722.
Park Ridge’s historic core includes several properties listed on the National Register of Historic Places, reflecting its rich architectural heritage.
Bernards High School was founded by the Bernards Township Board of Education in 1924, serving the local community for nearly a century.
